company.name: Nomadic Trails
company.description: We're a Mongolian inbound travel company, founded in 2004, started with a simple premise, bringing small groups to amazing locations. Showing our clients staggering scenery and interesting nomadic culture and to introduce them to the fascinating people that live among them. And it is this ethos that has seen our company grow and our clients return to us again.

testimonials.name: David
testimonials.text: From the very start, I felt in good hands. Our guide Taco was simply excellent. Every day brought something new—big skies, wide open land.

testimonials.name: Sarah
testimonials.text: Taco made the trip an extraordinarily enjoyable experience. Always very helpful and obliging.

testimonials.name: Jennifer
testimonials.text: I've been dreaming of horse archery training in Mongolia for years. Tulga was so supportive and attentive.

testimonials.name: Michael
testimonials.text: Our trip to Khovsgol was unforgettable! The breathtaking landscapes were truly mesmerizing.

testimonials.name: Tracy
testimonials.text: What a trip of a lifetime! It's epic, adventurous and very eye opening. Tulga planned a great itinerary.

testimonials.name: Eagle Festival Guest
testimonials.text: The Golden Eagle Festival was breathtaking! Watching the eagle hunters in action was a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

testimonials.name: Naadam Visitor
testimonials.text: Naadam was incredible! The wrestling, horse racing, and archery were unlike anything I've ever seen.

testimonials.name: Ice Festival Guest
testimonials.text: The Ice Festival on Lake Khuvsgul was magical. Walking on the frozen lake and seeing the ice sculptures was unforgettable.

testimonials.name: Eagle Hunter Visitor
testimonials.text: Visiting an eagle hunter family in the Altai Mountains was incredible. Learning about their traditions and seeing the eagles up close was unforgettable.

cultural_notes.horse_archery: Horseback archery is a traditional Mongolian sport dating back to the time of Genghis Khan. It requires exceptional skill and is one of the 'Three Manly Games' of the Naadam Festival. Practitioners train for years to master shooting targets while galloping at full speed.
cultural_notes.shaman: Mongolian shamanism (Böö mörgöl) is one of the oldest spiritual traditions in the world. Shamans serve as healers, spiritual guides, and mediators between the human and spirit worlds.
cultural_notes.gobi: The Gobi Desert is one of the world's most unique desert ecosystems, home to Bactrian camels, snow leopards, and the famous Flaming Cliffs where dinosaur fossils were first discovered.
cultural_notes.khuvsgul: Lake Khuvsgul is the 'Blue Pearl of Mongolia', the second largest freshwater lake in Asia by volume. It is sacred to local communities and home to the Tsaatan reindeer herders.
cultural_notes.altai: The Altai Mountains are a UNESCO World Heritage site, known for stunning landscapes, ancient petroglyphs, and the tradition of Kazakh eagle hunting.
cultural_notes.naadam: Naadam is Mongolia's biggest festival, featuring the 'Three Manly Games': wrestling, horse racing, and archery.
cultural_notes.eagle: Kazakh eagle hunting is an ancient tradition passed down through generations. Eagle hunters use trained golden eagles to hunt foxes and hares during winter.
cultural_notes.eagle_hunter: Kazakh eagle hunting is an ancient tradition passed down through generations in Mongolia's Altai Mountains. Eagle hunters (berkutchi) use trained golden eagles to hunt foxes and hares during winter.
cultural_notes.terelj: Terelj National Park features stunning granite rock formations like Turtle Rock, alpine meadows, and the famous Aryapala Meditation Center.
cultural_notes.genghis_khan_statue: The Genghis Khan Statue Complex is the world's largest equestrian statue, standing 40 meters tall, commemorating Mongolia's legendary founder.
cultural_notes.kharkhorin: Kharkhorin (Karakorum) was the ancient capital of the Mongol Empire. Today, it is home to Erdene Zuu Monastery, the first Buddhist monastery in Mongolia.
cultural_notes.ice_festival: The Ice Festival on Lake Khuvsgul takes place in March. Visitors can walk on the frozen lake, see ice sculptures, and experience traditional winter activities.
cultural_notes.khuvsgul_shaman: The Khuvsgul region, particularly the Darkhad Valley, is considered the spiritual heart of Mongolian shamanism.

4.title: Tsaatan Reindeer Tribe Experience - 4 Days
days: Flight to Murun (Monday, Wednesday, or Friday). Drive to Lake Khuvsgul. Visit local market for supplies. Overnight at ger camp.

Full day with Tsaatan reindeer herders. Participate in daily activities: herding reindeer, milking, setting up camp. Experience traditional shamanic practices. Overnight in teepee.
Return horseback ride from Tsaatan camp. Drive to Murun. Flight to Ulaanbaatar. Departure.

standard: Private 4x4 vehicle with experienced English-speaking guide and driver
All accommodations (hotels in Ulaanbaatar, ger camps in countryside)
All meals as per itinerary (breakfast, lunch, dinner)
All entrance fees and park permits
flight: Domestic flights (as required for your itinerary)
